name: CAP
description: Community-acquired pneumonia probability update using symptoms/vitals/exam + simplified CXR and labs. Starter LRs—replace with curated evidence.

pretestPresets.label: Primary Care
pretestPresets.notes: Setting-only baseline (not symptom-enriched). Add findings such as cough separately.

pretestPresets.label: Emergency Department
pretestPresets.notes: Setting-only baseline (not symptom-enriched). Add findings such as cough separately.

items.label: Purulent sputum
items.notes: Often non-specific.

items.label: CXR: lobar or multilobar consolidation/infiltrate
items.notes: If CXR done, mark Present/Absent. If not done, choose “CXR not done”.

items.label: CXR not done
items.notes: Neutral (does not change probability).

items.label: WBC ≥ 15,000
items.notes: Weak diagnostic modifier; more useful for severity than diagnosis in many contexts.

items.label: Procalcitonin elevated
items.notes: Assay-dependent; interpret with clinical context.

items.label: Respiratory viral panel positive
items.notes: Modeled as modifier of bacterial coinfection likelihood in CAP (proxy endpoint: blood-culture positivity). Derived from cohort rates (viral-positive 12.6%; blood-culture positivity 2.7% vs 5.3%), yielding LR+ ~0.53 and LR− ~1.07.

source.short: Klompas et al. Infect Control Hosp Epidemiol

items.label: Respiratory viral panel not done/unknown
items.notes: Neutral selection.

name: VAP
description: Ventilator-associated pneumonia (adult ICU, mechanically ventilated >48h) diagnostic probability update using clinical features and respiratory microbiology. Core LRs are derived from a 2020 systematic review/meta-analysis using histopathology as reference standard where available.

pretestPresets.label: ICU, mechanically ventilated >48 hours
pretestPresets.notes: Setting/time-only baseline. Select clinical, imaging, and microbiology findings separately. Calibrate to local ICU epidemiology and surveillance definitions.

source.short: Cook et al. Ann Intern Med

pretestPresets.label: ICU, mechanically ventilated >=5 days
pretestPresets.notes: Setting/time-only baseline with longer ventilation exposure. Higher duration mainly increases cumulative VAP risk and shifts pathogen resistance risk; calibrate locally.

source.short: Cook et al. Ann Intern Med

items.label: Fever (>=38 C)
items.notes: Weak diagnostic modifier in suspected VAP.

items.label: Purulent tracheal secretions
items.notes: Common but non-specific in mechanically ventilated ICU patients.

items.label: Chest radiograph infiltrate (new/progressive compatible opacity)
items.notes: High sensitivity but poor specificity in the meta-analysis. If CXR done, mark Present/Absent. If not done/indeterminate, choose 'CXR not done/unknown'.

items.label: CXR not done/unknown
items.notes: Neutral selection.

items.label: Leukocytosis (WBC >=12 x10^9/L)
items.notes: Evidence synthesis used varying thresholds (commonly >=10 or >=12 x10^9/L); this module standardizes to >=12 for usability. Weak diagnostic modifier.

items.label: Worsening oxygenation (PaO2/FiO2 <=240)
items.notes: Included because it is commonly used in bedside VAP suspicion frameworks, but evidence suggests poor diagnostic performance for microbiologically confirmed VAP. In one multicenter prospective cohort of suspected VAP, this threshold was associated with LESS microbiologic confirmation.

items.label: CPIS > 6 (use instead of individual CPIS component findings)
items.notes: Do not combine with fever/secretions/WBC/CXR component findings if using CPIS, as this will double count overlapping information.

items.label: CPIS not used/unknown
items.notes: Neutral selection.

items.label: Endotracheal aspirate culture positive
items.notes: Set Present for clinically significant ETA culture growth (quantitative threshold if your lab reports it; semiquantitative positive interpreted clinically if not). Published pooled LRs are based primarily on quantitative thresholds.

items.label: Protected specimen brush culture positive
items.notes: Set Present for clinically significant PSB culture growth. Published pooled LRs are based on quantitative thresholds (commonly >=10^3 CFU/mL).

items.label: BAL culture positive
items.notes: Set Present for clinically significant BAL culture growth (quantitative threshold if reported; semiquantitative positive interpreted clinically if not). Published pooled LRs are based primarily on quantitative thresholds (commonly >=10^4 CFU/mL).

items.label: Respiratory sampling/culture not done or unknown
items.notes: Neutral selection.

items.label: Serum procalcitonin elevated (diagnostic threshold assay/study dependent)
items.notes: Pooled HAP/VAP estimate from ATS/IDSA evidence review (cutoffs varied ~0.5-3.9 ng/mL). Not recommended as a sole trigger to start antibiotics.

items.label: Procalcitonin not done/unknown
items.notes: Neutral selection.

name: C. difficile
description: C. difficile infection probability update using diarrhea features + host/exposure risk + simplified NAAT→toxin testing. Starter LRs—replace with curated evidence.

pretestPresets.label: Outpatient / clinic unexplained diarrhea
pretestPresets.notes: Back-calculated low-acuity baseline before applying the stool-frequency LR, using an outpatient diarrhea cohort with about 3.9% toxin-positive CDI.

source.short: Kutty et al., Emerg Infect Dis

pretestPresets.label: ED / early admission diarrhea (first 72h)
pretestPresets.notes: Back-calculated ED or early-admission baseline before applying the stool-frequency LR, using an ED diarrhea cohort with about 10.2% CDI.

source.short: Abrahamian et al., Ann Emerg Med

pretestPresets.label: Hospital-onset diarrhea after day 3
pretestPresets.notes: Back-calculated hospital-onset baseline before applying the stool-frequency LR, using a suspected hospital-acquired diarrhea cohort with about 12.3% CDI.

items.label: Gross blood in stool
items.notes: Often suggests alternative dx (e.g., IBD flare, ischemia, invasive bacterial diarrhea).

items.label: WBC ≥ 15k
items.notes: More severity-associated than diagnostic.

items.label: Creatinine rise
items.notes: More severity-associated than diagnostic.

items.label: NAAT/PCR positive + Toxin EIA positive
items.notes: Most supportive of toxin-mediated CDI.

items.label: NAAT/PCR positive + Toxin EIA negative
items.notes: Could represent colonization or low toxin burden; interpret clinically.

items.label: NAAT/PCR positive (toxin not sent/unknown)
items.notes: Intermediate support; toxin result would refine.

name: UTI
description: UTI probability update using setting-only pretest probability plus symptoms, urinalysis (and optional culture), and host risk factors. Starter LRs—replace with curated evidence.

pretestPresets.label: Outpatient / primary care
pretestPresets.notes: Setting-only baseline (not symptom-enriched). Add symptoms/UA findings separately.

pretestPresets.label: ED / inpatient
pretestPresets.notes: Setting-only baseline (not symptom-enriched). Add symptoms/UA findings separately.

items.label: Vaginal discharge/irritation
items.notes: Suggests vaginitis/cervicitis rather than cystitis.

items.label: Male sex
items.notes: Lower likelihood of uncomplicated cystitis; consider prostatitis/complicated UTI.

name: Endocarditis
description: Infective endocarditis probability update using setting-only pretest probability plus selected host risk, organism-specific microbiology, risk-score context (VIRSTA / DENOVA / HANDOC), and imaging (TTE/TEE) ± FDG PET/CT. Imaging LRs derived from published sensitivity/specificity where available. Beware correlated Duke elements.

pretestPresets.label: Outpatient / primary care
pretestPresets.notes: Setting-only baseline. Add host, exam, microbiology, and imaging findings separately.

pretestPresets.label: ED / inpatient
pretestPresets.notes: Setting-only baseline. Add host, exam, microbiology, and imaging findings separately.

pretestPresets.label: Tertiary referral center
pretestPresets.notes: Setting-only baseline for referral settings. Add host, exam, microbiology, and imaging findings separately.

items.label: VIRSTA (SAB) score >=3
items.notes: High-sensitivity rule-out oriented SAB score for endocarditis risk stratification (external validation performance).

items.label: VIRSTA not applied/unknown
items.notes: Neutral selection.

items.label: DENOVA (E. faecalis bacteremia) >=3
items.notes: High sensitivity score for E. faecalis bacteremia endocarditis risk stratification.

items.label: DENOVA not applied/unknown
items.notes: Neutral selection.

items.label: HANDOC (NBHS bacteremia) >=3
items.notes: High sensitivity score for non-beta-hemolytic streptococcal bacteremia.

items.label: HANDOC not applied/unknown
items.notes: Neutral selection.

items.label: Blood cultures: Duke major (typical organism in ≥2 sets)
items.notes: Duke major criterion; avoid stacking with other major micro options.

items.label: Blood cultures: Duke major (persistent positivity)
items.notes: Duke major criterion; correlated with typical organism + echo findings.

items.label: Blood cultures: Staphylococcus aureus in >=2 sets
items.notes: Typical IE organism; choose one microbiology option to avoid double counting.

items.label: Blood cultures: Enterococcus faecalis in >=2 sets
items.notes: Typical organism in appropriate context; pair with DENOVA only if clinically justified.

items.label: Blood cultures: NBHS (e.g., viridans group / S. gallolyticus) in >=2 sets
items.notes: Typical organism in appropriate context; pair with HANDOC only if clinically justified.

items.label: Blood cultures: positive but NOT Duke major
items.notes: Example: single positive set, atypical organism, or uncertain significance.

items.label: Coxiella burnetii Phase I IgG ≥ 1:800
items.notes: Major criterion in IE guidelines; very specific for chronic Q fever endocarditis.

items.label: Transthoracic echo (TTE) with evidence of new vegetation, regurgitation or valve perforation
items.notes: LR derived from pooled sens/spec (meta-analysis). Performance lower in prosthetic/device/early disease.

items.label: Transesophageal echo (TEE) with evidence of new vegetation, regurgitation or valve perforation
items.notes: Pooled values for your target population (native vs prosthetic).

items.label: FDG PET/CT (prosthetic valve/device infection)
items.notes: Best for prosthetic valve/device IE; negative does not exclude native-valve IE.

name: Active TB
description: Active pulmonary TB probability update using setting-only pretest probability plus selected host risk factors, symptom screen strategy, immune-based tests (QFT/TST), respiratory microbiology (MTB PCR, AFB smear, culture), and chest X-ray. LRs use pooled estimates where available.

pretestPresets.label: Outpatient
pretestPresets.notes: Setting-only baseline. Add host/risk findings separately below.

pretestPresets.label: ED / Inpatient
pretestPresets.notes: Setting-only baseline. Add host/risk findings separately below.

items.label: Close/household contact with infectious pulmonary TB
items.notes: Approximate risk enrichment from contact-investigation cohorts/meta-analyses.

items.label: Born in or prolonged travel/residence in high-incidence TB region
items.notes: Epidemiologic risk enrichment modeled conservatively to avoid overcounting with other host factors.

items.label: Current or recent incarceration
items.notes: Prison populations have much higher TB burden than the general population; LR kept conservative due overlap with other social/host risks.

source.short: Cords et al. Lancet Public Health

items.label: Experiencing homelessness or regular shelter exposure
items.notes: Homeless populations/shelters are higher-risk TB settings; LR kept conservative to reduce overlap with other vulnerabilities.

source.short: Beijer et al. Lancet Infect Dis

items.label: HIV or major cellular immunosuppression
items.notes: Major TB risk factor; modeled as conservative pretest enrichment in this tool.

items.label: Diabetes mellitus
items.notes: Epidemiologic risk modifier rather than a direct microbiologic diagnostic test.

items.label: Any WHO TB symptom (cough, fever, night sweats, or weight loss)
items.notes: From pooled sensitivity/specificity of symptom screening studies.

items.label: Cough >=2 weeks (cough-only symptom strategy)
items.notes: Higher specificity than broad symptom screens, with lower sensitivity.

items.label: Symptom screen not done/unknown
items.notes: Neutral selection.

items.label: QuantiFERON (IGRA) result
items.notes: Set Present=positive, Absent=negative. Supportive only for active TB diagnosis.

items.label: Tuberculin skin test (TST) result
items.notes: Set Present=positive, Absent=negative. Supportive only for active TB diagnosis.

items.label: QFT/TST not done/unknown
items.notes: Neutral selection.

items.label: MTB PCR (Xpert MTB/RIF) on sputum
items.notes: Present=positive PCR, Absent=negative PCR. Pooled sensitivity ~85%, specificity ~98%.

items.label: MTB PCR (Xpert MTB/RIF) on BAL
items.notes: Present=positive PCR, Absent=negative PCR. Pooled PLR 10.21, NLR 0.16.

source.short: Liu et al. J Clin Microbiol

items.label: AFB smear microscopy (sputum)
items.notes: Present=smear positive, Absent=smear negative. Pooled sensitivity ~64%, specificity ~98%.

source.short: Davis et al. Lancet Infect Dis

items.label: Mycobacterial culture (sputum)
items.notes: Present=culture positive, Absent=culture negative. Reported specificity ~100%; LR+ is mathematically non-finite (capped here at 100).

items.label: Mycobacterial culture (BAL)
items.notes: Present=culture positive, Absent=culture negative. Reported specificity ~100%; LR+ is mathematically non-finite (capped here at 100).

items.label: CXR suggestive of active pulmonary TB
items.notes: Set Present=suggestive CXR, Absent=not suggestive CXR.

items.label: CXR not done/unknown
items.notes: Neutral selection.

name: PJP
description: Pneumocystis jirovecii pneumonia probability update using setting-only pretest probability plus selected host risk factors, vitals, serum BDG, respiratory diagnostics (PCR/DFA), and chest imaging.

pretestPresets.label: Outpatient / Emergency Department
pretestPresets.notes: Setting-only baseline. Add host/risk findings separately below.

source.short: Mappin-Kasirer et al. BMC Infect Dis

pretestPresets.label: Hospitalized (non-ICU)
pretestPresets.notes: Setting-only baseline. Add host/risk findings separately below.

source.short: Mappin-Kasirer et al. BMC Infect Dis

pretestPresets.label: ICU
pretestPresets.notes: Setting-only baseline. Add host/risk findings separately below.

source.short: Mappin-Kasirer et al. BMC Infect Dis

items.label: HIV (CD4 <=200) or solid organ transplant
items.notes: Approximate LR informed by adjusted OR from a bronchoscopy-derived diagnostic cohort.

source.short: Mappin-Kasirer et al. BMC Infect Dis

items.label: No TMP-SMX prophylaxis despite indication
items.notes: Absence of prophylaxis increases risk; prophylaxis is strongly protective in risk-score cohorts.

source.short: Mappin-Kasirer et al. BMC Infect Dis

items.label: Prolonged systemic steroids (roughly >=20 mg prednisone-equivalent for >=3 weeks)
items.notes: Commonly recognized major risk state for non-HIV PJP; treated as conservative pretest enrichment.

items.label: Hypoxemia
items.notes: Weak diagnostic discriminator alone in multivariable diagnostic cohorts.

source.short: Mappin-Kasirer et al. BMC Infect Dis

items.label: Fever
items.notes: Common but non-specific in immunocompromised hosts.

source.short: Mappin-Kasirer et al. BMC Infect Dis

items.label: Serum beta-D-glucan (BDG)
items.notes: Set Present=positive, Absent=negative. Good rule-out utility when pretest probability is low/intermediate.

source.short: Del Corpo et al. Clin Microbiol Infect

items.label: Serum BDG not done/unknown
items.notes: Neutral selection.

items.label: Serum LDH elevated (around >=265 IU/L)
items.notes: OR-informed approximation from multivariable diagnostic model.

source.short: Mappin-Kasirer et al. BMC Infect Dis

items.label: Respiratory PCR (BAL qPCR)
items.notes: Set Present=positive, Absent=negative.

source.short: Brown et al. Clin Infect Dis

items.label: Respiratory PCR (induced sputum qPCR)
items.notes: Set Present=positive, Absent=negative.

source.short: Brown et al. Clin Infect Dis

items.label: Respiratory PCR (upper airway sample)
items.notes: Set Present=positive, Absent=negative.

source.short: Brown et al. Clin Infect Dis

items.label: Respiratory PCR not done/unknown
items.notes: Neutral selection.

items.label: Respiratory direct fluorescent antibody (DFA/IFA)
items.notes: Set Present=positive, Absent=negative. Very specific but substantially less sensitive than PCR in routine cohorts.

items.label: Respiratory DFA/IFA not done/unknown
items.notes: Neutral selection.

items.label: CXR typical of PJP (diffuse/interstitial-interstitial-alveolar pattern)
items.notes: Approximation informed by pooled ORs and multivariable diagnostic cohorts.

source.short: Wills et al. Open Forum Infect Dis

items.label: CT typical of PJP (ground-glass/interstitial pattern)
items.notes: OR-informed approximation from multivariable CT diagnostic cohorts.

source.short: Mappin-Kasirer et al. BMC Infect Dis

items.label: Chest imaging not done/unknown
items.notes: Neutral selection.

name: Invasive Candidiasis
description: Invasive candidiasis probability update using care-setting pretest probability plus selected risk factors (host findings), serum biomarkers, molecular diagnostics, and culture-based confirmation.

pretestPresets.label: Outpatient / Emergency Department
pretestPresets.notes: Setting-only baseline (low prevalence). Add host/risk findings separately below.

source.short: Clancy and Nguyen. Clin Infect Dis

pretestPresets.label: Hospitalized (non-ICU)
pretestPresets.notes: Setting-only baseline. Add host/risk findings separately below.

source.short: Clancy and Nguyen. Clin Infect Dis

pretestPresets.label: ICU
pretestPresets.notes: Setting-only baseline (typical ICU incidence is generally low single-digit percentages). Add host/risk findings separately below.

items.label: Total parenteral nutrition
items.notes: Candida Score component; modeled as an OR-informed risk enricher rather than a standalone diagnostic test.

source.short: León et al. Crit Care Med

items.label: Recent surgery
items.notes: Candida Score component; modeled as an OR-informed risk enricher rather than a standalone diagnostic test.

source.short: León et al. Crit Care Med

items.label: Multifocal Candida colonization
items.notes: Candida Score component; stronger enrichment when colonization is documented at multiple non-blood sites.

source.short: León et al. Crit Care Med

items.label: Severe sepsis/septic shock
items.notes: Candida Score component with the highest weighting in the original score derivation.

source.short: León et al. Crit Care Med

items.label: Serum beta-D-glucan (BDG)
items.notes: Pan-fungal biomarker; values derived from pooled IFI performance and interpreted as supportive (not Candida-specific).

source.short: Karageorgopoulos et al. Clin Infect Dis

items.label: Serum BDG not done/unknown
items.notes: Neutral selection.

items.label: Combined mannan + anti-mannan assay
items.notes: Set Present=positive combined assay, Absent=negative combined assay.

items.label: Mannan/anti-mannan testing not done/unknown
items.notes: Neutral selection.

items.label: T2Candida panel
items.notes: Set Present=positive, Absent=negative. High NPV performance in pooled analyses.

source.short: Tang et al. BMC Infect Dis

items.label: T2Candida not done/unknown
items.notes: Neutral selection.

items.label: Candida PCR from blood
items.notes: Set Present=positive, Absent=negative.

source.short: Avni et al. J Clin Microbiol

items.label: Candida PCR not done/unknown
items.notes: Neutral selection.

items.label: Blood/sterile-site culture positive for Candida
items.notes: Strong rule-in finding. A negative blood culture should not be used to exclude invasive candidiasis.

items.label: Culture strategy not done/unknown
items.notes: Neutral selection.

name: Invasive Mold Infection
description: Invasive mold infection probability update using care-setting pretest probability plus selected host risk factors, chest imaging, serum/BAL fungal biomarkers, Aspergillus PCR, and Mucorales PCR. Current diagnostics are Aspergillus-dominant with dedicated Mucorales PCR support.

pretestPresets.label: Outpatient / Emergency Department
pretestPresets.notes: Setting-only baseline (very low prevalence). Add host/risk findings separately below.

pretestPresets.label: Hospitalized (non-ICU)
pretestPresets.notes: Setting-only baseline. Add host/risk findings separately below.

pretestPresets.label: ICU
pretestPresets.notes: Setting-only baseline (non-enriched ICU population). Add host/risk findings separately below.

source.short: Feys et al. Lancet Infect Dis

items.label: Profound neutropenia or recent allogeneic HSCT
items.notes: Consensus major host factor for invasive mold disease; modeled as pretest enrichment.

source.short: Donnelly et al. Clin Infect Dis

items.label: Active hematologic malignancy (e.g., AML/MDS/relapsed leukemia)
items.notes: Consensus host-risk enricher for invasive aspergillosis and related mold disease.

source.short: Donnelly et al. Clin Infect Dis

items.label: Solid organ transplant recipient
items.notes: Modeled as a conservative pretest-risk enrichment factor.

items.label: Prolonged corticosteroids or significant T-cell immunosuppression
items.notes: Modeled as a conservative host-risk enricher when classic immunosuppression is present.

source.short: Donnelly et al. Clin Infect Dis

items.label: Severe viral pneumonia in ICU with corticosteroid exposure (CAPA context)
items.notes: Host/context enrichment only; add diagnostics (GM/PCR/imaging) for meaningful rule-in or rule-out shifts.

source.short: Feys et al. Lancet Infect Dis

items.label: Persistent/refractory fever despite broad-spectrum antibacterials
items.notes: Supportive but non-specific in high-risk patients.

items.label: Chest CT halo sign (nodular lesion with surrounding ground-glass opacity)
items.notes: Good rule-in support in the right host context; absence does not exclude invasive mold infection.

source.short: Agarwal et al. Eur J Radiol

items.label: Chest CT not done/unknown
items.notes: Neutral selection.

items.label: Serum galactomannan (ODI >0.5)
items.notes: Set Present=positive, Absent=negative. LR values are calculated from pooled sensitivity 0.92 and specificity 0.84 for the commonly used serum ODI >0.5 cutoff in hematologic populations.

items.label: BAL galactomannan (ODI >1.0)
items.notes: Set Present=positive, Absent=negative. LR values are calculated from pooled sensitivity 0.75 and specificity 0.96 for the commonly used BAL ODI >1.0 cutoff in hematologic populations.

items.label: Galactomannan testing not done/unknown
items.notes: Neutral selection.

items.label: Serum beta-D-glucan (BDG)
items.notes: Pan-fungal biomarker; interpret with clinical context and potential non-mold causes of positivity.

source.short: Huang et al. Clin Respir J

items.label: Serum BDG not done/unknown
items.notes: Neutral selection.

items.label: Aspergillus lateral flow test (LFD/LFA)
items.notes: Set Present=positive, Absent=negative. Interpret by specimen type and local assay performance.

items.label: Aspergillus LFD/LFA not done/unknown
items.notes: Neutral selection.

items.label: Aspergillus PCR from BAL
items.notes: Set Present=positive, Absent=negative. Performance can vary with antifungal exposure and assay method.

source.short: Avni et al. J Clin Microbiol

items.label: Respiratory culture grows Aspergillus (BAL preferred; sputum less specific)
items.notes: Supportive rule-in microbiology, especially when recovered from BAL in a compatible host and imaging context. This LR is conservatively modeled from BAL culture sensitivity 0.40 and specificity 0.903; sputum or other lower-respiratory cultures are more colonization-prone and a negative culture should not be used to exclude invasive mold disease.

items.label: Respiratory fungal culture not done/unknown
items.notes: Neutral selection.

items.label: Aspergillus PCR from plasma
items.notes: Set Present=positive, Absent=negative. LR values are calculated from sensitivity 94.7% and specificity 83.3% in a standardized plasma PCR study; evidence is less mature than BAL-based Aspergillus PCR.

source.short: White et al. J Clin Microbiol

items.label: Aspergillus PCR strategy not done/unknown
items.notes: Neutral selection.

items.label: Mucorales PCR from BAL
items.notes: Set Present=positive, Absent=negative. Very strong negative LR in pooled analyses.

source.short: Brown et al. Int J Infect Dis

items.label: Mucorales PCR from blood
items.notes: Set Present=positive, Absent=negative.

source.short: Brown et al. Int J Infect Dis

items.label: Mucorales PCR not done/unknown
items.notes: Neutral selection.

name: Septic Arthritis
description: Native-joint septic arthritis probability update using setting-only pretest probability plus host risk factors, exam findings, inflammatory markers, synovial fluid testing, microbiology, and supportive imaging.

pretestPresets.label: Outpatient monoarthritis evaluation
pretestPresets.notes: Setting-only baseline. Add host factors, arthrocentesis data, and microbiology separately below.

pretestPresets.label: ED acute atraumatic monoarthritis
pretestPresets.notes: Setting-only baseline for an acute native-joint presentation before synovial testing is incorporated.

pretestPresets.label: Inpatient / consultative workup
pretestPresets.notes: Higher-acuity baseline for admitted or specialty-consult cases where septic arthritis is already a leading concern.

items.label: Age >80 years
items.notes: Modeled as a modest baseline risk enricher.

items.label: Diabetes mellitus
items.notes: Baseline host-risk enrichment for native-joint septic arthritis.

items.label: Rheumatoid arthritis or inflammatory arthropathy
items.notes: Inflammatory joint disease increases septic arthritis risk and complicates bedside discrimination.

items.label: Meaningful immunosuppression
items.notes: Includes chemotherapy, transplant immunosuppression, high-dose steroids, or comparable immune compromise.

items.label: Injection drug use
items.notes: Modeled as a modest hematogenous-seeding risk enricher.

items.label: Recent joint surgery, injection, or penetrating trauma
items.notes: Local inoculation risk factor; use for native-joint contexts rather than prosthetic-joint infection.

items.label: Concurrent bacteremia or overlying skin/soft-tissue infection
items.notes: Hematogenous or contiguous-source context that materially raises baseline suspicion.

items.label: Acute painful swollen native joint / monoarthritis
items.notes: Core clinical syndrome descriptor; supportive but not specific without synovial testing.

items.label: Fever (>=38 C)
items.notes: Absence of fever does not exclude septic arthritis.

items.label: Marked pain with passive or active range of motion
items.notes: Useful bedside discriminator when motion is clearly limited by pain.

source.short: Carpenter et al. Acad Emerg Med

items.label: Warmth, erythema, or clinically apparent effusion
items.notes: Supportive joint exam finding with limited standalone specificity.

items.label: C-reactive protein elevated
items.notes: Set Present=above local threshold, Absent=below threshold.

source.short: Carpenter et al. Acad Emerg Med

items.label: ESR elevated
items.notes: Set Present=above local threshold, Absent=below threshold.

source.short: Carpenter et al. Acad Emerg Med

items.label: Synovial WBC >=50,000/uL
items.notes: Use Present when the aspirate is at or above this threshold and Absent when it is below.

source.short: Carpenter et al. Acad Emerg Med

items.label: Arthrocentesis cell count not done/unknown
items.notes: Neutral selection.

source.short: Carpenter et al. Acad Emerg Med

items.label: Synovial PMN >=90%
items.notes: Helpful supportive marker when differential is available.

source.short: Carpenter et al. Acad Emerg Med

items.label: Synovial Gram stain positive
items.notes: Set Present=organisms seen, Absent=negative Gram stain.

source.short: Carpenter et al. Acad Emerg Med

items.label: Synovial Gram stain not done/unknown
items.notes: Neutral selection.

source.short: Carpenter et al. Acad Emerg Med

items.label: Synovial fluid culture positive
items.notes: Set Present=positive synovial culture, Absent=negative culture.

source.short: Shirtliff and Mader. Am Fam Physician

items.label: Synovial fluid culture not done/unknown
items.notes: Neutral selection.

source.short: Shirtliff and Mader. Am Fam Physician

items.label: Blood culture positive with plausible matching pathogen
items.notes: Best used when the bloodstream isolate plausibly explains the joint infection.

items.label: Blood cultures not done/unknown
items.notes: Neutral selection.

items.label: Ultrasound or advanced imaging shows joint effusion
items.notes: Helpful when aspiration is difficult or deep-joint effusion needs confirmation; not specific for infection.

items.label: Joint imaging not done/unknown
items.notes: Neutral selection.

name: Bacterial Meningitis
description: Adult bacterial meningitis probability update using setting-only pretest probability plus baseline risk factors, bedside findings, CSF profile, microbiology, and limited supportive neuroimaging.

pretestPresets.label: Outpatient or low-acuity evaluation
pretestPresets.notes: Low baseline before strong meningeal, CSF, or microbiologic evidence is added.

pretestPresets.label: ED suspected meningitis
pretestPresets.notes: Baseline for an ED presentation where meningitis is part of the active differential but not yet microbiologically confirmed.

source.short: van de Beek et al. N Engl J Med

pretestPresets.label: Inpatient / ICU / specialty consult
pretestPresets.notes: Higher baseline for admitted or specialty-evaluated cases where bacterial meningitis is already a leading concern.

items.label: Age >=50 years
items.notes: Modeled as a modest baseline risk enricher in adult bacterial meningitis.

source.short: van de Beek et al. N Engl J Med

items.label: Meaningful immunocompromise
items.notes: Includes transplant, chemotherapy, high-dose steroids, advanced immunodeficiency, or comparable host compromise.

items.label: CSF leak, neurosurgery, CNS device, or recent skull-base trauma
items.notes: Strong structural predisposition to bacterial meningitis.

items.label: Concurrent otitis, mastoiditis, or sinusitis
items.notes: Contiguous ENT source that modestly enriches the pretest probability.

source.short: van de Beek et al. N Engl J Med

items.label: Concurrent bacteremia or sepsis
items.notes: Hematogenous context materially raises suspicion for bacterial meningitis.

items.label: Headache
items.notes: Common but non-specific; useful mainly in combination with fever, neck stiffness, and mental-status change.

items.label: Fever (>=38 C)
items.notes: Absence lowers probability but does not exclude meningitis.

items.label: Neck stiffness / meningismus
items.notes: Supportive exam finding with imperfect sensitivity.

items.label: Altered mental status
items.notes: Part of the classic adult presentation cluster.

items.label: Petechial or purpuric rash
items.notes: Helpful rule-in clue for meningococcal disease when present.

items.label: New seizure
items.notes: Supportive high-acuity neurologic feature.

source.short: van de Beek et al. N Engl J Med

items.label: Serum procalcitonin elevated
items.notes: Useful bacterial-vs-aseptic discriminator when clearly elevated.

source.short: Viallon et al. Clin Infect Dis

items.label: CSF WBC >=1,000/uL
items.notes: Use Present when CSF pleocytosis is at or above this threshold and Absent when below.

items.label: CSF cell count not done/unknown
items.notes: Neutral selection.

items.label: CSF neutrophils >=80%
items.notes: Helpful supportive CSF profile feature.

items.label: CSF:serum glucose ratio <0.4 or CSF glucose low
items.notes: Strong bacterial meningitis discriminator when clearly low.

items.label: CSF protein elevated
items.notes: Supportive but less specific than CSF glucose and microbiology.

items.label: CSF lactate elevated
items.notes: Strong bacterial-vs-aseptic discriminator when available.

items.label: CSF Gram stain positive
items.notes: Very strong rule-in finding when organisms are seen.

items.label: CSF Gram stain not done/unknown
items.notes: Neutral selection.

items.label: CSF culture positive
items.notes: Set Present=positive CSF culture, Absent=negative culture.

items.label: CSF culture not done/unknown
items.notes: Neutral selection.

items.label: Blood culture positive with plausible bacterial meningitis pathogen
items.notes: Helpful when the bloodstream isolate plausibly explains the CNS infection.

source.short: van de Beek et al. N Engl J Med

items.label: Blood cultures not done/unknown
items.notes: Neutral selection.

source.short: van de Beek et al. N Engl J Med

items.label: CSF bacterial PCR / multiplex panel positive
items.notes: Set Present when a bacterial target is detected on a validated CSF molecular assay.

items.label: CSF bacterial PCR not done/unknown
items.notes: Neutral selection.

items.label: Neuroimaging supportive of meningitis or complications
items.notes: Weak supportive finding; diagnosis remains primarily clinical plus CSF and microbiology.

items.label: Neuroimaging not done/unknown
items.notes: Neutral selection.

name: Encephalitis
description: Adult infectious encephalitis probability update using baseline risk factors, mental-status and seizure features, CSF findings, HSV-weighted molecular testing, MRI, and EEG.

pretestPresets.label: Low-acuity neuro evaluation
pretestPresets.notes: Low baseline before strong CSF, MRI, EEG, or PCR evidence is added.

pretestPresets.label: ED suspected encephalitis
pretestPresets.notes: Baseline for an acute ED presentation with possible infectious encephalitis before LP, MRI, or PCR confirmation.

pretestPresets.label: Inpatient / ICU / specialty consult
pretestPresets.notes: Higher baseline for admitted or specialty-evaluated cases where infectious encephalitis is already a leading concern.

items.label: Meaningful immunocompromise
items.notes: Raises the baseline probability of infectious encephalitis and broadens the pathogen differential.

items.label: Transplant recipient or major biologic/immunomodulator exposure
items.notes: Modeled as a modest baseline risk enricher for opportunistic and viral encephalitides.

items.label: Relevant mosquito, tick, travel, or animal exposure
items.notes: Epidemiologic enricher for arboviral and zoonotic encephalitis pathways.

items.label: Fever
items.notes: Supportive infectious feature; absence lowers probability but does not exclude encephalitis.

items.label: Altered mental status / encephalopathy
items.notes: Core encephalitis syndrome feature in adults.

items.label: Behavioral, memory, or personality change
items.notes: Helpful limbic/temporal-lobe clue in compatible presentations.

items.label: New focal neurologic deficit
items.notes: Supportive but not specific; best interpreted with MRI and CSF findings.

items.label: New seizure
items.notes: Common high-acuity feature in infectious encephalitis, especially HSV-compatible disease.

items.label: CSF pleocytosis
items.notes: Pleocytosis supports encephalitis but may be absent early or in immunocompromised patients.

items.label: CSF cell count not done/unknown
items.notes: Neutral selection.

items.label: CSF protein elevated
items.notes: Supportive but not specific.

items.label: CSF RBC elevated / hemorrhagic CSF pattern
items.notes: HSV-weighted clue; interpret cautiously if the LP was traumatic.

items.label: CSF HSV PCR positive
items.notes: Very strong HSV encephalitis signal; a negative result does not fully exclude disease early in the course.

source.short: Binnicker et al. J Clin Virol

items.label: CSF HSV PCR not done/unknown
items.notes: Neutral selection.

source.short: Binnicker et al. J Clin Virol

items.label: Other CSF viral PCR / multiplex panel positive
items.notes: Use when a clinically plausible non-HSV viral target is detected in CSF.

items.label: Other CSF viral PCR not done/unknown
items.notes: Neutral selection.

items.label: MRI with temporal / frontotemporal / insular abnormality compatible with encephalitis
items.notes: Heavily HSV-weighted neuroimaging clue; MRI is more useful than CT for early diagnostic support.

items.label: Brain MRI not done/unknown
items.notes: Neutral selection.

items.label: EEG with temporal slowing or periodic discharges compatible with encephalitis
items.notes: Supportive electrophysiologic clue, particularly in HSV-compatible presentations.

items.label: EEG not done/unknown
items.notes: Neutral selection.

name: Spinal Epidural Abscess
description: Spinal epidural abscess probability update using baseline risk factors, back pain and neurologic features, inflammatory markers, blood cultures, and MRI-based confirmation.

pretestPresets.label: Outpatient back pain evaluation
pretestPresets.notes: Very low baseline before major risk factors, inflammatory markers, or MRI evidence are added.

source.short: Arko et al. Surg Neurol Int

pretestPresets.label: ED severe back pain / possible SEA
pretestPresets.notes: Baseline for emergency presentations where SEA is being actively considered but not yet imaged.

source.short: Davis et al. J Emerg Med

pretestPresets.label: Inpatient / neuro-spine consult
pretestPresets.notes: Higher baseline for admitted or specialty-evaluated cases with concerning risk factors or neurologic findings.

source.short: Arko et al. Surg Neurol Int

items.label: Injection drug use
items.notes: Important hematogenous-seeding risk factor for SEA.

source.short: Arko et al. Surg Neurol Int

items.label: Diabetes mellitus
items.notes: Common baseline risk factor in SEA cohorts.

source.short: Arko et al. Surg Neurol Int

items.label: Meaningful immunocompromise
items.notes: Includes transplant, chemotherapy, advanced immunodeficiency, or high-dose steroids.

source.short: Arko et al. Surg Neurol Int

items.label: Recent spinal procedure, epidural injection, or spinal hardware
items.notes: Major local inoculation or postoperative risk context.

source.short: Arko et al. Surg Neurol Int

items.label: Concurrent bacteremia or skin/soft-tissue infection
items.notes: Supports hematogenous or contiguous spread.

source.short: Davis et al. J Emerg Med

items.label: Severe focal back or neck pain
items.notes: Core syndrome feature but not specific.

source.short: Davis et al. J Emerg Med

items.label: Fever
items.notes: Absence of fever does not exclude SEA.

source.short: Davis et al. J Emerg Med

items.label: Midline spinal tenderness
items.notes: Useful bedside clue in the right clinical context.

source.short: Davis et al. J Emerg Med

items.label: New focal neurologic deficit or weakness
items.notes: High-acuity clue supporting cord or nerve-root involvement.

source.short: Arko et al. Surg Neurol Int

items.label: Urinary retention or bowel/bladder dysfunction
items.notes: Concerning late neurologic feature that raises urgency and probability.

source.short: Arko et al. Surg Neurol Int

items.label: ESR markedly elevated
items.notes: Modeled after evidence that low ESR materially lowers the probability of SEA.

source.short: Davis et al. J Emerg Med

items.label: CRP elevated
items.notes: Supportive inflammatory marker with better utility when interpreted alongside ESR and MRI.

source.short: Davis et al. J Emerg Med

items.label: Peripheral WBC elevated
items.notes: Weak standalone discriminator.

source.short: Davis et al. J Emerg Med

items.label: Blood culture positive with plausible SEA pathogen
items.notes: Best used when the bloodstream isolate plausibly explains the spinal infection.

source.short: Arko et al. Surg Neurol Int

items.label: Blood cultures not done/unknown
items.notes: Neutral selection.

source.short: Arko et al. Surg Neurol Int

items.label: MRI spine showing epidural abscess or phlegmon
items.notes: MRI is the key rule-in and rule-out modality when performed over the relevant symptomatic region(s).

source.short: Tetsuka et al. Neurol Med Chir

items.label: MRI spine not done/unknown
items.notes: Neutral selection.

source.short: Tetsuka et al. Neurol Med Chir

items.label: Imaging also suggests vertebral osteomyelitis or discitis
items.notes: Supportive adjacent-spine infection clue rather than a standalone SEA test.

source.short: Arko et al. Surg Neurol Int

name: Brain Abscess
description: Pyogenic brain abscess probability update using contiguous-source and hematogenous risk factors, focal neurologic features, inflammatory markers, microbiology, and neuroimaging with MRI-weighted evidence.

pretestPresets.label: Low-acuity neurologic evaluation
pretestPresets.notes: Very low baseline before major source risk factors, focal deficits, or diagnostic neuroimaging are added.

source.short: Brouwer et al. Curr Opin Infect Dis

pretestPresets.label: ED focal headache / lesion workup
pretestPresets.notes: Baseline for emergency presentations where abscess is in the differential but imaging or microbiology is not yet confirmatory.

source.short: Brouwer et al. Curr Opin Infect Dis

pretestPresets.label: Inpatient / neuro / neurosurgery consult
pretestPresets.notes: Higher baseline for admitted or specialty-evaluated cases with a plausible infectious source or compatible imaging.

source.short: Helweg-Larsen et al. Open Forum Infect Dis

items.label: Contiguous ENT or dental source (otitis, mastoiditis, sinusitis, dental infection)
items.notes: Common predisposing source for pyogenic brain abscess.

source.short: Helweg-Larsen et al. Open Forum Infect Dis

items.label: Recent neurosurgery, cranial hardware, or penetrating head trauma
items.notes: Major local inoculation risk context.

source.short: Brouwer et al. Curr Opin Infect Dis

items.label: Concurrent endocarditis, bacteremia, or other hematogenous source
items.notes: Supports hematogenous seeding to the brain.

source.short: Helweg-Larsen et al. Open Forum Infect Dis

items.label: Meaningful immunocompromise
items.notes: Raises baseline probability while broadening the pathogen differential.

source.short: Brouwer et al. Curr Opin Infect Dis

items.label: Headache
items.notes: Common but non-specific symptom.

items.label: Fever
items.notes: Absent fever does not exclude brain abscess.

items.label: New focal neurologic deficit
items.notes: Strong clue when paired with compatible imaging.

source.short: Helweg-Larsen et al. Open Forum Infect Dis

items.label: Altered mental status / confusion
items.notes: Supportive high-acuity neurologic feature.

items.label: Seizure
items.notes: Can be an initial manifestation, especially for cortical lesions.

source.short: Helweg-Larsen et al. Open Forum Infect Dis

items.label: CRP elevated
items.notes: Supportive inflammatory marker with limited specificity.

source.short: Brouwer et al. Curr Opin Infect Dis

items.label: Peripheral WBC elevated
items.notes: Weak standalone discriminator.

source.short: Brouwer et al. Curr Opin Infect Dis

items.label: Blood culture positive with plausible brain abscess pathogen
items.notes: Most useful when paired with a compatible source and imaging findings.

source.short: Helweg-Larsen et al. Open Forum Infect Dis

items.label: Blood cultures not done/unknown
items.notes: Neutral selection.

source.short: Helweg-Larsen et al. Open Forum Infect Dis

items.label: Abscess aspirate or operative culture positive
items.notes: Very strong rule-in evidence when a compatible organism is recovered.

source.short: Helweg-Larsen et al. Open Forum Infect Dis

items.label: Abscess aspirate culture not done/unknown
items.notes: Neutral selection.

source.short: Helweg-Larsen et al. Open Forum Infect Dis

items.label: MRI with ring-enhancing lesion and restricted diffusion compatible with abscess
items.notes: MRI with DWI is the preferred imaging discriminator for pyogenic abscess.

items.label: Brain MRI not done/unknown
items.notes: Neutral selection.

source.short: Brouwer et al. Curr Opin Infect Dis

items.label: Contrast CT with ring-enhancing lesion compatible with abscess
items.notes: Helpful when MRI is unavailable, but less specific than MRI with DWI.

source.short: Brouwer et al. Curr Opin Infect Dis

items.label: Brain CT not done/unknown
items.notes: Neutral selection.

source.short: Brouwer et al. Curr Opin Infect Dis

items.label: Multiple lesions or surrounding cerebritis / edema supportive of infection
items.notes: Supportive imaging context rather than a standalone diagnostic test.

source.short: Brouwer et al. Curr Opin Infect Dis

name: Necrotizing Soft Tissue Infection
description: Necrotizing soft tissue infection probability update using source and host risk factors, pain and rapid progression, systemic toxicity, key laboratory abnormalities, microbiology, and CT or MRI findings.

pretestPresets.label: Low-acuity soft tissue infection evaluation
pretestPresets.notes: Very low baseline before major red flags, laboratory abnormalities, or imaging evidence are added.

pretestPresets.label: ED severe soft tissue infection / possible NSTI
pretestPresets.notes: Baseline for emergency presentations where NSTI is under active consideration but not yet confirmed operatively or radiographically.

pretestPresets.label: Inpatient / surgical consult
pretestPresets.notes: Higher baseline for admitted or surgical-consult cases with major red flags or rapidly progressive disease.

items.label: Diabetes mellitus
items.notes: Common host risk factor in NSTI cohorts.

items.label: Meaningful immunocompromise
items.notes: Broad host-risk enricher for invasive and rapidly progressive soft tissue infection.

items.label: Injection drug use
items.notes: Local inoculation and polymicrobial infection risk context.

items.label: Recent surgery, wound, trauma, or injection at the site
items.notes: Major local inoculation and postoperative risk context.

items.label: Perineal source, pressure injury, ulcer, or chronic wound
items.notes: Contiguous source risk factor, including Fournier-type presentations.

items.label: Pain out of proportion to exam
items.notes: High-yield bedside clue when clearly present.

items.label: Rapid progression over hours to 1-2 days
items.notes: Supportive clue for invasive necrotizing disease rather than routine cellulitis.

items.label: Fever
items.notes: Absence of fever does not exclude NSTI.

items.label: Hypotension or shock
items.notes: Highly specific but insensitive systemic toxicity marker.

items.label: Hemorrhagic bullae, skin necrosis, or ecchymosis
items.notes: Specific but late finding.

items.label: Crepitus or subcutaneous gas on exam
items.notes: Specific clue but often absent early.

items.label: Cutaneous anesthesia or sensory loss over the lesion
items.notes: Supportive clue for fascial and nerve involvement.

items.label: CRP markedly elevated
items.notes: Adjunctive inflammatory marker; useful in combination with exam and imaging.

items.label: Peripheral WBC elevated
items.notes: Weak standalone discriminator.

items.label: Hyponatremia
items.notes: Supportive LRINEC-adjacent clue; should not be used alone to rule in or rule out NSTI.

items.label: Lactate elevated
items.notes: Supportive systemic toxicity marker.

items.label: Blood culture positive with plausible NSTI pathogen
items.notes: Helpful when paired with compatible clinical or imaging findings.

items.label: Blood cultures not done/unknown
items.notes: Neutral selection.

items.label: Operative exploration shows dishwater fluid, necrotic fascia, or easy fascial dissection
items.notes: Near-definitive rule-in evidence when classic operative findings are present.

items.label: Operative exploration not done/unknown
items.notes: Neutral selection.

items.label: CT with gas, deep fascial fluid, or inflammatory changes crossing fascial planes
items.notes: Best interpreted using combined NSTI-compatible CT findings rather than gas alone.

items.label: CT not done/unknown
items.notes: Neutral selection.

items.label: MRI with deep fascial T2 hyperintensity or enhancement compatible with NSTI
items.notes: MRI is sensitive but less specific than definitive operative findings.

items.label: MRI not done/unknown
items.notes: Neutral selection.

name: PJI
description: Prosthetic joint infection probability update using setting-only pretest probability plus selected host risk factors, exam findings, inflammatory markers, synovial biomarkers, microbiology, and supportive imaging.

pretestPresets.label: Outpatient arthroplasty clinic follow-up
pretestPresets.notes: Setting-only baseline. Add host/exam/lab findings separately below.

source.short: Cortes-Penfield et al. Clin Infect Dis

pretestPresets.label: ED / inpatient
pretestPresets.notes: Setting-only baseline. Add host/exam/lab findings separately below.

source.short: Cortes-Penfield et al. Clin Infect Dis

pretestPresets.label: Pre-revision arthroplasty evaluation
pretestPresets.notes: Setting-only baseline for revision workup contexts. Add host/exam/lab findings separately below.

items.label: Prior revision arthroplasty
items.notes: Approximate enrichment from pooled observational risk-factor data.

source.short: Zhu et al. Int Wound J

items.label: Obesity (roughly BMI >=30)
items.notes: Modeled as a modest pretest risk enricher.

source.short: Zhu et al. Int Wound J

items.label: Diabetes mellitus
items.notes: Modeled as a modest pretest risk enricher.

source.short: Zhu et al. Int Wound J

items.label: Rheumatoid arthritis or meaningful immunosuppression
items.notes: Risk-factor enrichment from pooled observational datasets.

source.short: Zhu et al. Int Wound J

items.label: Persistent or worsening pain in prosthetic joint
items.notes: Common but non-specific; best interpreted with labs and aspiration data.

source.short: Cortes-Penfield et al. Clin Infect Dis

items.label: Fever (>=38 C)
items.notes: Absent fever does not exclude PJI, especially chronic presentations.

source.short: Cortes-Penfield et al. Clin Infect Dis

items.label: Local erythema, warmth, swelling, or drainage
items.notes: Physical exam finding; supportive but not definitive without microbiologic correlation.

source.short: Cortes-Penfield et al. Clin Infect Dis

items.label: Sinus tract communicating with prosthesis
items.notes: Major diagnostic criterion with very high rule-in value.

items.label: C-reactive protein elevated
items.notes: Set Present=above selected threshold, Absent=below threshold.

items.label: ESR elevated
items.notes: Set Present=above selected threshold, Absent=below threshold.

items.label: Synovial alpha-defensin (ELISA laboratory assay)
items.notes: Set Present=positive, Absent=negative. Very strong rule-in performance in pooled analyses.

items.label: Synovial alpha-defensin (lateral flow)
items.notes: Set Present=positive, Absent=negative. Point-of-care format with lower sensitivity vs ELISA.

items.label: Synovial leukocyte esterase strip
items.notes: Set Present=positive, Absent=negative. Strong diagnostic utility in pooled analyses.

items.label: Synovial biomarker testing not done/unknown
items.notes: Neutral selection.

items.label: Preoperative synovial fluid culture
items.notes: Set Present=positive, Absent=negative.

items.label: Intraoperative periprosthetic tissue culture
items.notes: Set Present=positive, Absent=negative.

items.label: Sonication fluid culture (removed prosthesis)
items.notes: Set Present=positive, Absent=negative.

items.label: Culture strategy not done/unknown
items.notes: Neutral selection.

items.label: Synovial PCR for bacterial detection
items.notes: Set Present=positive, Absent=negative.

items.label: Synovial PCR not done/unknown
items.notes: Neutral selection.

items.label: Plain radiograph suggestive of infection (rapid loosening/osteolysis/periosteal reaction)
items.notes: Supportive finding with limited standalone discrimination.

source.short: Cortes-Penfield et al. Clin Infect Dis

items.label: Imaging not done/unknown
items.notes: Neutral selection.

name: Tuberculous uveitis
description: Consensus-based tuberculous uveitis assessment for ophthalmology visits using the Collaborative Ocular Tuberculosis Study (COTS) calculator inputs: lesion phenotype, TB endemicity, TST, IGRA/QuantiFERON, and chest imaging. The primary output is a mapped consensus probability for initiating antitubercular therapy, not a microbiologic gold-standard disease probability.

pretestPresets.label: Ophthalmology visit
pretestPresets.notes: Conservative fallback pretest used only when phenotype is still missing or no override is provided. Once phenotype is known, the starting pretest is derived from the phenotype/endemicity COTS baseline rather than this fallback anchor.

items.label: Anterior uveitis, first episode
items.notes: COTS phenotype: anterior uveitis, first episode.

items.label: Anterior uveitis, recurrent episode
items.notes: COTS phenotype: anterior uveitis, recurrent episode.

items.label: Patient from TB-endemic region
items.notes: High-burden TB country / endemic region as used by the COTS calculator.

items.label: Tuberculin skin test not done / unknown
Chest X-ray / CT positive for healed or active TB signs
items.notes: COTS treats any healed or active TB sign on chest X-ray or chest CT as positive.

items.label: Chest X-ray / CT negative for healed or active TB signs
Chest X-ray / CT not done / unknown
Prior TB disease or known latent TB infection
items.notes: Pretest-only modifier: general TB epidemiologic context calibrated for ocular TB, not part of the COTS matrix.

items.label: Close TB contact or household TB exposure
items.notes: Pretest-only modifier: general TB contact-risk context calibrated for ocular TB, not part of the COTS matrix.

items.label: Macular or other vision-threatening lesion
items.notes: Harm-only modifier: lowers the treatment threshold because delayed therapy risks central vision loss.

items.label: Bilateral disease or only-seeing eye
items.notes: Harm-only modifier: lowers the treatment threshold because a missed diagnosis carries higher visual disability risk.

items.label: Progressive vision loss, severe inflammation, or steroid-dependent course
items.notes: Harm-only modifier: lowers the treatment threshold when the ocular course is vision-threatening.

items.label: Host immunosuppression
items.notes: Harm-only modifier: lowers the treatment threshold because missed ocular TB may progress more aggressively.

items.label: Major ATT hepatotoxicity risk
items.notes: Harm-only modifier: raises the treatment threshold when empiric ATT is riskier than usual.

items.label: Mild chronic liver disease risk (for example Child-Pugh A or MELD-Na under 10)
items.notes: Harm-only modifier: mild chronic liver disease raises ATT treatment harm modestly.

items.label: Moderate chronic liver disease risk (for example Child-Pugh B or MELD-Na 10-19)
items.notes: Harm-only modifier: moderate chronic liver disease raises ATT treatment harm substantially.

items.label: Severe chronic liver disease risk (for example Child-Pugh C or MELD-Na 20 or higher)
items.notes: Harm-only modifier: severe chronic liver disease raises ATT treatment harm markedly.

items.label: Higher ethambutol ocular-toxicity risk
items.notes: Harm-only modifier: raises the treatment threshold when baseline optic-neuropathy risk is higher than usual (for example older age, renal dysfunction, or prior optic neuropathy).

items.label: Major rifamycin interaction, prior ATT intolerance, or resistance concern
items.notes: Harm-only modifier: raises the treatment threshold when ATT is logistically or biologically riskier than usual.
